claims.claim: Excited about Stablecoins & onchain payments as crypto's clearest PMF and the rails layer, aided by clearer US regulation
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about AI x crypto convergence (agent-to-agent payments, proof-of-personhood/verifiable identity, decentralized compute)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about DePIN — token incentives bootstrapping real-world hardware networks (compute, wireless, energy, storage)
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Excited about onchain UX primitives (account & chain abstraction; hiding keys/gas/bridging) as the binding adoption constraint
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about extractive memecoin & launchpad speculation as negative-sum and damaging to crypto's credibility
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about reflexive/circular DeFi leverage and points-farming inflating headline TVL
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Neutral risk regime: no near-term directional price view, structurally long on a 7–10yr horizon, constructive on fundamentals
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Developed an AI-for-security / anti-doomer thesis (May 13 podcast + tweets): AI doesn't create new vulnerabilities, only finds existing ones; defenders should massively adopt AI to red-team themselves; explicitly criticizes Anthropic 'doomsday marketing' and calls AI fear 'completely backwards' — not anticipated by the prediction

Articulated a detailed token value-capture / tokenomics framework (Feb 8, Apr 23–27, May 14): buy-and-burn is good but protocols must 'print-and-spend' to grow, decoupling fees from issuance, DUNA entities; 'tokens need a value capture mechanism' and pro-revenue/fees stance

Took a new DeFi operational-security stance (Mar 25, May 13): advocates rate-limited mint/withdraw and time-delayed admin actions, and argues recent hacks stemmed from inadvertent centralization — 'more decentralization, not less' — a concern axis distinct from the predicted memecoin/leverage worries

Showed token/sector-specific enthusiasm for Zcash and privacy tech (Feb 18–Mar 5, Jun 5): ZSAs/shielded assets 'inevitable' and 'incredibly important,' client-side privacy zkVMs, praising Zcash devs — not anticipated by the deliberately empty token arrays
Strong agentic-engineering / AI-coding enthusiasm ('vibe coding ended, agentic engineering began'; excitement for AI-built games/apps) — adjacent to AI x crypto but a distinct AI-tooling stance
notes: Strongest confirmations come from the May 5 a16z Crypto Fund 5 essay (stablecoins as the 'clearest evidence,' GENIUS Act/regulation tailwind, cycle-agnostic long-horizon fundamentals view, swarms of agents transacting) and the May 2 memecoin post where he explicitly reaffirms and claims vindication for his 'memecoins are damaging' stance. AI x crypto is confirmed across many posts (x402, AgentCash/AgentKit, World Face Auth, 'human verification aided by cryptography will be scarce'). Risk regime confirmed: he consistently avoids near-term price calls and frames conviction as long-horizon/structural (launching a $2.2B fund 'for this moment'). Onchain UX primitives marked confirmed but with nuance — his engagement is via passkey/key-portability, self-custody, interop (LayerZero) and bridging (ZCash) UX; note he pushes toward user *control* over pure abstraction, a slightly different flavor than the prediction's 'hide it all from users.' DePIN is absent — only a vague 'decentralized infrastructure' list item in the essay, with no engagement of the hardware/token-incentive thesis. Reflexive/circular DeFi leverage is absent — his DeFi commentary centers on security/hacks and tokenomics value-capture, not levered TVL or points farming. No contradictions found.
